name: Kefir (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Kefir [Milk and milk products] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170904 (Kefir, lowfat, plain, LIFEWAY) - sugars, protein, minerals, water-soluble vitamins
USDA FoodData Central FDC 172217 / 746782 (Milk, whole 3.25%) - fatty acid profile, cholesterol, fat-soluble vitamins
USDA whole cow milk amino-acid profile (SR) - amino acids
Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000, J Dairy Sci 83(2):275 (PMID 10714860) - organic acids (lactic up to 7739 ppm, citric, pyruvic, orotic)
Prado et al. 2015, Front Microbiol 6:1177 (Milk kefir: composition, microbial cultures) - lactose hydrolysis, ethanol, acetic acid, CO2
McCance & Widdowson (UK) - chloride, iodine, biotin milk values
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are lactose (~3.7 g) plus its hydrolysis products glucose+galactose (~0.9 g) AND lactic acid (~0.8 g) - the last was entirely missing from the FooDB profile and is the defining metabolite of this acid-alcoholic fermented milk. Ethanol, acetic acid, citric and orotic acids are the other key organic-acid/co-product additions. Basis is whole-milk kefir; lowfat kefir has ~1 g fat (fatty acids and cholesterol scale down ~3x, other values similar). Cholesterol was RETAIN
curation_added: L-lactic acid (DOMINANT fermentation metabolite, ~0.8 g/100g - the single biggest omission)
D-glucose and D-galactose (from lactose hydrolysis; galactose accumulates)
quantitative amounts for all previously name-only amino acids, fatty acids, minerals and vitamins

curation_removed.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
curation_removed.reason: Plain kefir contains no sucrose; only sweetened/flavored kefir does. FooDB carry-over, not a component of the plain fermented-milk medium.

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: Plant furanocoumarin (celery/citrus); biologically implausible in a cow-milk product. FooDB predicted noise.

curation_removed.name: epsilon-Polylysine
curation_removed.reason: Microbial preservative/food additive, not an intrinsic kefir nutrient. FooDB carry-over.

curation_removed.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
curation_removed.reason: Trace aroma volatile, not a growth nutrient; FooDB flavor-compound listing.

curation_removed.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine
curation_removed.reason: Predicted non-physiological stereoisomer; the real component is L-isoleucine (retained).

curation_removed.name: 24,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3
curation_removed.reason: Predicted vitamin D catabolite at ultratrace level; not a meaningful growth nutrient.

curation_removed.name: Co2+ (free cobalt ion)
curation_removed.reason: Ultratrace/predicted; biologically relevant cobalt is bound in vitamin B12, which is retained. Free ionic cobalt is FooDB noise.

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: Ultratrace element, not a defined milk growth nutrient; FooDB trace-element noise.

curation_removed.name: Chromium
curation_removed.reason: Ultratrace element; not a growth-defining nutrient of kefir.

curation_removed.name: Nonanoate (C9:0)
curation_removed.reason: Odd-chain C9 fatty acid essentially absent from bovine milk fat; FooDB artifact (milk FAs are even-chain 4:0-18:x).

curation_removed.name: Molybdate
curation_removed.reason: Ultratrace; not a quantified growth-relevant milk nutrient. Dropped as noise (kept out of mineral panel).

components.name: water
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170904 (kefir, lowfat, plain, LIFEWAY); milk matrix

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170904 (total sugars 4.61 minus lactose 3.70) + Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177 (lactose hydrolysis)

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170904 total-minus-lactose pool + Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177

components.name: L-lactic acid
components.evidence_source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 J Dairy Sci 83:275 (PMID 10714860; up to 7739 ppm = 0.77 g/100g by d21)

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence_source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 J Dairy Sci 83:275 (PMID 10714860); milk citrate ~0.16 g/100g

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence_source: Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177; kefir reviews (acetic-acid bacteria + heterofermentation)

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.evidence_source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 (PMID 10714860; pyruvic produced during fermentation)

components.name: oleic acid
components.evidence_source: USDA whole milk 3.25% (mono total 0.69, mostly oleic)

uncovered.name: orotic acid
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
basis: per 100 g whole-milk kefir (dairy, acid-alcoholic fermented cow milk)
